title: Pathology Slide Scanner Workflow
des_qualification_note: All required criteria satisfied: discrete slides flow through the scanner; discrete events (slide loaded, scan start and end, QC check, unload, route to safety container); explicit simulated clock across shifts or 24-hour operation. Both typical criteria present: resource contention across finite scanner capacity (480 slides per batch), rack slots (20 or 30 per rack), operator availability, and safety container capacity; scan time varies by sample (40 seconds nominal but depends on sample complexity) and QC flagging is stochastic.
summary_of_content_provenance: Vendor-anchored entry using the 3DHISTECH Pannoramic 480 (P480) as representative of the high-throughput clinical scanner class. Two first-party fetches succeeded: the 3DHISTECH P480 product page (First-party x Direct x Background — vendor marketing content, not simulation methodology) and the PMC lab automation review PMC11062949 (First-party x Adjacent x Background — review paper describing lab equipment and workflow, not specifically scanner DES). One second-party direct DES source from search snippets: Springer batch scheduling paper (paywalled). Two fetch attempts failed and would live in a deferred human_followup_queue: Cambridge WSC 2024 PDF (PDF text extraction failure — a new friction pattern that first appeared in this walkthrough) and UCL Discovery histopathology simulation thesis (403 bot-block). The blueprint references multiple scanner brands (P480, Aperio GT 450, Agilent S540MD, NanoZoomer, Motic EasyScan Infinity, Grundium Ocus) but these vendor product references currently have no structured home in the schema.
